task_objective: Swap the per-game disabled status strip icon to the Lucide save-off glyph (lu/LuSaveOff), following on-device confirmation of the per-game sync toggle feature.
tests_added: Updated the existing per-game disabled notice test to assert the Lucide glyph: 24x24 viewBox, the 'm2 2 20 20' slash stroke, and stroke="currentColor" so the amber .icon colour still applies. Red was confirmed before the implementation edit.
design_decisions: The status strip is standalone HTML injected into a Steam browser view, not React, so icons are raw SVG strings. The react-icons component cannot be used; the glyph geometry was transcribed from the authoritative react-icons definition rather than hand-drawn.

No dependency bump. This repo pins react-icons 5.3.0, which predates LuSaveOff (it exposes only LuSave and LuSaveAll); geometry was taken from react-icons 5.6.0 already present on this machine. Because the consumer is a raw string, upgrading the package was unnecessary.

Upstream's 7th path ('M29.5 11.5s5 5 4 5') was omitted: it begins beyond the 24-wide viewBox and renders nothing. The omission is recorded in a code comment so the divergence from upstream is not mistaken for a transcription error.
The glyph is stroke-based while sibling icons are fill-based; stroke="currentColor" keeps it inside the existing amber colour group, so no CSS change was needed.
The test-expectation change is required by the requested behavior change (the icon itself is the deliverable), not a test edited to mask an unrelated failure.

results.automated: Targeted suite green (28 tests), then full quality gates: pnpm test, pnpm run build, ruff check/format, ty, pytest.
results.scope_note: The requested auto-hide extension (2s to 3s) was dropped at the user's direction after it was found to be a single shared constant (RESULT_HIDE_DELAY_MS) governing every terminal status strip rather than the disabled notice alone.
results.on_device: The preceding per-game sync toggle feature was confirmed on hardware from pulled Deck logs: sync_disabled_games_count at hydration, game_sync_disabled skip on both start and exit, status published then auto-hidden on start, explicit hide on exit with no lingering strip, no pause or Syncthing watch, and correct app-ID resolution from the Steam launch name to the Ludusavi canonical name. This icon change itself is not yet visually confirmed on device.
